name: Residential Proxies
tagline: Real home IPs that blend in anywhere
definition: Residential proxies route your traffic through real IP addresses assigned by consumer internet service providers to everyday home users. Because targets see a genuine ISP-issued residential IP rather than a server subnet, these proxies deliver the highest trust score for web scraping, ad verification, and accessing geo-restricted content. They are the go-to choice when a site aggressively blocks datacenter ranges.
howItWorks: Requests are relayed through a large pool of residential devices, each carrying an authentic ISP IP tied to a real city and carrier. A gateway or backconnect endpoint assigns you an exit IP, optionally rotating it on every request or holding a sticky session for several minutes. To the destination server the connection looks identical to an ordinary household visitor.
pros: Extremely high trust and low block rates on protected sites
Precise geo-targeting down to country, state, and city
Millions of IPs make bans and rate limits hard to enforce
Ideal for sneaker, ticketing, and social platforms that filter datacenter IPs
Sticky sessions available for multi-step, login-based workflows
cons: Priced per gigabyte, so bandwidth-heavy jobs get expensive fast
Slower and less consistent than datacenter or ISP proxies
Uptime depends on real devices that can drop off the network
Ethical sourcing varies between providers, so vet the vendor carefully
bestFor: Large-scale web scraping of protected targets
poolNote: Pools typically span tens of millions of IPs sourced from consenting home users across most countries and carriers.

name: Datacenter Proxies
tagline: Fast, cheap IPs for high-volume tasks
definition: Datacenter proxies are IP addresses hosted on servers in commercial data centers rather than tied to a home internet subscription. They are the fastest and most affordable proxy type, making them ideal for bandwidth-heavy scraping, SEO monitoring, and any task where raw throughput matters more than blending in. The tradeoff is that their IP ranges are easy for sophisticated sites to identify and block.
howItWorks: Your traffic passes through IPs owned by hosting providers and cloud platforms, delivered as either shared or dedicated addresses. Because these IPs sit on server subnets with predictable ASN ownership, they route with very low latency but also carry a recognizable non-residential fingerprint. Rotation and subnet diversity are used to spread requests and avoid rate limits.
pros: Fastest speeds and lowest latency of any proxy type
Cheapest cost per IP, often billed as flat monthly rate
Effectively unlimited bandwidth on most plans
Dedicated options give you exclusive, predictable IPs
Great for high-concurrency, non-sensitive scraping at scale
cons: Easily detected and blocked by anti-bot systems and major platforms
Shared IPs can arrive pre-flagged from other users' abuse
Limited geo-diversity compared with residential pools
Not suitable for sneaker sites, social media, or strict targets
bestFor: SEO rank tracking and keyword research
Bulk API access and data aggregation
poolNote: Pools are built from server subnets across major hosting providers and are far smaller and less diverse than residential networks.

name: Mobile Proxies
tagline: Carrier IPs with unbeatable trust scores
definition: Mobile proxies route traffic through IP addresses issued by cellular carriers to real 3G, 4G, and 5G devices. Because carriers share a small number of IPs across thousands of subscribers via CGNAT, these IPs are almost impossible to ban without collateral damage, giving them the highest trust score of any proxy type. They are the premium choice for the most aggressively protected platforms.
howItWorks: Your requests exit through mobile devices or SIM-based modems connected to real cellular towers, presenting a carrier-assigned IP. Carrier-grade NAT means many legitimate users share each address, so blocking it risks blocking real customers. Many providers offer on-demand IP rotation by cycling the mobile connection, which hands you a fresh carrier IP in seconds.
pros: Highest trust score and lowest ban rate of any proxy type
CGNAT makes IP bans impractical for target sites to enforce
Fresh carrier IP on demand via connection rotation
Excellent for social media, ad platforms, and app testing
IPs appear as genuine smartphone traffic
cons: Most expensive proxy type, often billed per port or dedicated modem
Speeds and stability vary with cellular signal and congestion
Smaller concurrent capacity per port than datacenter setups
Limited fine-grained city targeting on some carrier networks
bestFor: Ad verification on mobile ad networks
App store and mobile app testing
Accessing the most heavily protected targets
Account creation and verification at scale
poolNote: Pools are sourced from real SIM cards and mobile devices across major carriers, with each IP shared among many subscribers via CGNAT.

name: ISP Proxies
definition: ISP proxies, also called static residential proxies, are IP addresses registered under a consumer internet service provider but hosted on fast servers in a data center. This hybrid gives you the residential trust score that targets respect combined with the raw speed and stability of a datacenter connection. Because they are static and dedicated, they hold a single trusted IP for as long as you need it.
howItWorks: The provider acquires IP blocks that are registered to residential ISPs at the ASN level, then hosts them on high-bandwidth servers. Targets performing a lookup see a legitimate residential ISP owner, while you get near-datacenter latency and 24/7 uptime. Since each IP is dedicated and unchanging, it is perfect for long-lived logged-in sessions.
pros: Residential-level trust with datacenter-level speed
Static, dedicated IPs stay stable for weeks or months
No per-gigabyte metering, so heavy usage stays predictable
Consistent uptime unlike device-dependent residential pools
Ideal for warming and maintaining persistent accounts
cons: Smaller, less diverse pools than rotating residential networks
Higher per-IP cost than plain datacenter proxies
A burned static IP must be replaced rather than rotated away
Limited city-level geo options compared with residential
bestFor: Sneaker and retail checkout with persistent sessions
Stable scraping where the same IP is an advantage
Ad account and payment platform operations
poolNote: Pools are modest in size, built from ISP-registered IP blocks hosted on servers and assigned as dedicated static addresses.

name: Rotating Proxies
tagline: A new IP on every request, automatically
definition: Rotating proxies automatically assign a different IP address from a large pool on each request or at set time intervals, rather than binding you to one fixed IP. Delivered through a single backconnect gateway, they let you spread thousands of requests across countless IPs to sidestep rate limits and IP bans. Rotation is a delivery method that can sit on top of residential, mobile, or datacenter pools.
howItWorks: You connect to one static gateway endpoint, and the provider transparently cycles your exit IP behind it, drawing from its underlying pool. Rotation can trigger per request for maximum diversity or hold a sticky session for a chosen duration when a workflow needs continuity. This shifts IP management off your code and onto the provider's infrastructure.
pros: Automatic IP diversity defeats rate limits and soft bans
Single gateway endpoint simplifies integration
Scales effortlessly to millions of requests
Sticky-session option preserves continuity when needed
Works across residential, mobile, or datacenter backends
cons: Mid-session rotation can break multi-step or login flows
Less control over which specific IP you land on
Residential-backed rotation is billed per gigabyte
Occasional slow or previously flagged exit IPs
bestFor: Search engine and SERP data collection
Price and inventory monitoring at volume
poolNote: Rotation draws from the provider's underlying pool, which may range from millions of residential IPs to a smaller datacenter set.

name: SOCKS5 Proxies
definition: SOCKS5 refers to a proxy protocol rather than an IP source, tunneling any kind of TCP or UDP traffic at a lower level than HTTP proxies. Because it does not interpret or rewrite your data, SOCKS5 handles everything from web browsing and torrents to gaming, streaming, and email with support for authentication and UDP. It can run over residential, datacenter, or ISP IPs depending on the provider.
howItWorks: A SOCKS5 proxy establishes a raw tunnel between your client and the destination, forwarding packets without inspecting the application-layer payload. This protocol-agnostic design supports both TCP and UDP, plus username and password authentication for secure access. The result is lower overhead and broader compatibility than HTTP-only proxies, since almost any networked application can point at a SOCKS5 endpoint.
pros: Handles any protocol, not just HTTP and HTTPS traffic
Supports UDP for streaming, gaming, and VoIP
Lower overhead since payloads are not parsed or altered
Built-in authentication for secure, private access
Works with a huge range of apps and tools
cons: No traffic encryption by itself, so pair it with TLS or a VPN
Trust and speed depend entirely on the underlying IP source
Fewer app-layer features than HTTP proxies, such as header control
Some clients and tools only support HTTP proxying
poolNote: Availability and pool size depend on whether the provider layers SOCKS5 over residential, ISP, or datacenter IPs.

name: IPv6 Proxies
tagline: Massive cheap IP volume for IPv6 targets
definition: IPv6 proxies use the newer 128-bit IPv6 address space instead of the exhausted IPv4 pool, giving providers access to an almost limitless supply of IPs at very low cost. This makes them ideal for high-volume tasks against targets that fully support IPv6, where you can rotate through enormous subnets cheaply. The catch is that many sites are still IPv4-only, so IPv6 proxies only work where the destination speaks IPv6.
howItWorks: Providers allocate huge IPv6 blocks, often entire /48 or /64 subnets, and hand out addresses from a practically inexhaustible range. Your requests exit on IPv6 IPs, which only reach destinations that publish an IPv6 (AAAA) record. Because a single subnet contains billions of addresses, per-IP rotation is trivial and extremely cheap compared with scarce IPv4 supply.
pros: Enormous IP supply enables aggressive, cheap rotation
Lowest cost per IP thanks to IPv6 address abundance
Whole subnets available for large-scale distributed jobs
Fast datacenter-grade speeds and low latency
Excellent value for IPv6-ready scraping targets
cons: Only works on sites that support IPv6, still a minority
Some targets treat entire IPv6 subnets as one entity for blocking
Lower trust score than residential or mobile IPs
Not a fit for IPv4-only platforms and services
bestFor: Bulk account and registration tasks where supported
poolNote: Pools are effectively unlimited, carved from vast IPv6 subnets, but usefulness is capped by how many targets support IPv6.
